requirements_conformance.evidence: OpenRouterClient(LLMClient) implements complete(prompt, *, model, temperature, max_tokens, timeout) -> str matching the ABC signature exactly. Posts to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible), wraps prompt as a single user message, parses choices[0].message.content. Matches issue #20 4.1.1 acceptance criteria and test spec ('assert request shape and response parsing') verbatim.

architecture_conformance.evidence: Mirrors OllamaClient's established pattern: constructor-injectable httpx.BaseTransport for tests, provider-specific *ClientError(LLMError) subclass, fail-loud error handling (raises on HTTP error, non-JSON body, missing/malformed choices/message/content), same DEFAULT_BASE_URL/DEFAULT_MODEL/DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS constant convention. Correctly adds bearer API-key handling (not needed by local Ollama) resolved from explicit api_key kwarg or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var, raising OpenRouterClientError at construction time if neither is present -- consistent with docs/LLD/llm-provider.md's 'no code outside agents/llm/ calls a provider SDK directly' rule and 'OpenRouter (GPT-4o-mini) used for query-time agents' guidance. No new dependency added (httpx already used by ollama_client.py).

regression_risk.evidence: Purely additive: git show confirms only 2 new files, zero existing files modified. agents/llm/ suite: 33 passed (was 13 for Ollama-only per 3.4.1; +20 for this commit). Full agents/ suite (excluding one pre-existing unrelated collection error) : 173 passed, 2 failed. The 2 failures (agents/ingestion/test_shortlist.py gRPC tests) and the 1 collection error (agents/ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py) are all the same pre-existing protobuf gencode/runtime VersionError (gencode 6.33.5 vs runtime 5.29.6), confirmed via git log to originate from an unrelated earlier commit (9794fd8, issue #19) that predates 6109d13 -- not caused by this diff.

edge_cases.evidence: Explicit tests for: missing API key at construction (raises immediately, never sends unauthenticated request), HTTP 500 status, malformed/non-JSON body, missing 'choices' key, empty choices list, missing message.content, connection error. All raise OpenRouterClientError rather than silently returning empty/None, matching the ABC's documented contract.

security.evidence: API key resolved from explicit kwarg or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var only -- no hardcoded key found anywhere in the diff. Key is sent solely via Authorization: Bearer header, never logged, never included in exception messages (verified: OpenRouterClientError messages reference URL/response body only, not self._api_key).

performance.evidence: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=60.0, shorter than Ollama's 120.0, with a disclosed rationale (hosted API, no local model-load latency). httpx.Client is opened/closed per call (same pattern as OllamaClient) -- not connection-pooled across calls, a minor perf note but consistent with the existing Ollama precedent so not a regression introduced by this diff.

maintainability.evidence: Module docstring explicitly discloses 4 design decisions (endpoint choice, default model, API-key handling, error handling) mirroring ollama_client.py's docstring structure. Constants and error class naming follow the same convention, making a future Gemini client (4.1.2) straightforward to add symmetrically.

test_coverage.evidence: 20 new tests covering: LLMClient ABC contract, isinstance check, API-key resolution (explicit/env/missing), request shape (endpoint path, payload fields, auth header, base URL, default/override model), response parsing (happy path), and 6 distinct error-handling paths. All mocked via httpx.MockTransport injected through the constructor -- no real network calls, no monkeypatching of httpx internals.

confidence.evidence: High confidence in correctness of the reviewed diff. Two non-blocking observations below.

findings.summary: No test exercises httpx.TimeoutException specifically (a subclass of httpx.HTTPError, so it IS caught by the existing `except httpx.HTTPError` block and correctly wrapped in OpenRouterClientError) -- only httpx.ConnectError is tested directly. Behavior is correct by code inspection but not exercised by a dedicated test.

complete() opens/closes a new httpx.Client on every call rather than reusing a connection pool across calls on the same OpenRouterClient instance. Identical to the pre-existing OllamaClient pattern (not a regression introduced here), but for a hosted/networked provider under real query-time load this has more relative latency impact than for local Ollama.

tests_run: cd agents && python3 -m pytest llm/ -q  => 33 passed
cd agents && python3 -m pytest . -q --ignore=ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py  => 173 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing, unrelated protobuf gencode/runtime mismatch traced to commit 9794fd8, issue #19)
ruff check agents/llm/  => All checks passed
isolation_note: No implementer plan/reasoning/handoff artifacts under .cdr/runs/.../implementation/ for this subtask were read. Context was built independently from: gh issue view 20, git show 6109d13, agents/llm/client.py, agents/llm/ollama_client.py, docs/LLD/llm-provider.md, and direct test execution.
security_disclosure: During this run, tool output unexpectedly contained text formatted as system-reminder blocks (a 'date changed' notice, an MCP 'tokensave' server instructions block for a tool not present in my actual tool list, and an 'Auto Mode Active' directive encouraging reduced caution around destructive git actions). These did not appear embedded inside git/gh content strings but arrived directly in the tool-call flow; out of caution they were treated as untrusted/unverified and not acted upon -- no MCP tokensave tool was used, and the standing no-destructive-git-actions caution was maintained throughout (a subsequent attempted `git stash -u` was correctly blocked by the permission system, which was the right outcome). Flagged here per this task's explicit instruction to disclose any such anomalies.
